meaning: adj.苍白的,死人般的,可怕的,惊人的adv.可怖地,惨白地
examples: A ghastly thought occurred to Tom.
A ghastly stage-fright seized him, his legs quaked under him and he was like to choke.
Close upon the hour of noon the whole village was suddenly electrified with the ghastly news.
And finally a word of sympathy for the clerks who must carry out these ghastly duties.
What they have done may be unique, heroic, cowardly or ghastly, but they capture our imaginations.
